---
title: "Nikita Palatkin"
sortby: "2"
subtitle: "Doctoral Researcher"
image: nikita_crop3.JPG
started: "Nov 2024"
toc: false
about:
  id: person-profile
  template: jolla

---

```{=html}
<nav aria-label="breadcrumb">
  <ol class="breadcrumb">
    <li class="breadcrumb-item"><a href="../../index.html">Home</a></li>
    <li class="breadcrumb-item"><a href="../../people.html">People</a></li>
    <li class="breadcrumb-item active">Research Staff</li>
    <li class="breadcrumb-item active" aria-current="page">{{< meta title >}}</li>
  </ol>
</nav>
```

:::{#person-profile}
:::

## Research Interests

- 4D-STEM imaging and computational tomography
- Ptychographic reconstruction and phase retrieval
- Physics-informed neural networks for microscopy
- Signal processing and inverse problems

## Background

Nikita joined the ECLIPSE Lab as a doctoral researcher in November 2024, co-funded through his PhD position at the University of Canterbury, New Zealand.

He holds a BSc in Physics (2020, Honours) and an MSc in Physics (2022) from **Novosibirsk State University**, Russia. Alongside his MSc, he completed a two-year professional retraining programme in **Software Engineering and Data Science** at the Computer Science Center.

Prior to joining ECLIPSE, Nikita worked as a Research Engineer at **Motive Neuromorphic Technologies** (2022–present), developing algorithms and software for neuromorphic AI accelerators (AltAI 2 and AltAI 3). He previously held research positions at the International Tomography Center SB RAS (2019–2021) and the Institute of Chemical Kinetics and Combustion SB RAS (2021–2022).

## Experience

- **ECLIPSE Lab, FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg** · Doctoral Researcher · Since Nov 2024
- **University of Canterbury** · PhD (ongoing) · School of Physical and Chemical Sciences
- **LLC Motive Neuromorphic Technologies** · Research Engineer · Aug 2022–Jul 2024
- **Institute of Chemical Kinetics and Combustion SB RAS** · Engineer · Nov 2021–Jul 2022
- **International Tomography Center SB RAS** · Junior Researcher · Sep 2019–Sep 2021

## Technical Skills

- **Programming:** Python, C, C++
- **Deep learning:** PyTorch, TensorFlow 2, PyG, Transformers
- **Computer vision:** OpenCV
- **Spiking networks:** snnTorch, Nengo, NengoDL
- **ML:** scikit-learn, CatBoost
- **Other:** NumPy, SciPy, Pandas, PostgreSQL, Docker, Linux, Git
